basic_details.job_title: NIT Srinagar Temporary Faculty Recruitment 2026: Vacancies, Eligibility, Salary & How to Apply
basic_details.short_information: National Institute of Technology (NIT) Srinagar has released Advertisement No. NITS/Temporary Faculty/2026/01(F) dated 12 June 2026, inviting applications from Indian nationals for the engagement of Temporary Teaching Staff across multiple departments. The recruitment drive covers more than 80 vacancies in Engineering, Sciences, and Humanities, offering a fixed monthly remuneration of ₹65,000 for an 11-month temporary tenure. The last date to apply is 28 June 2026, with applications to be submitted via email to the respective department's designated ID.

qualification.details: This section explains the minimum educational qualifications required for each department, which is essential information for candidates checking their eligibility before applying. | In general, candidates must hold a Bachelor's degree (B.E./B.Tech) in the relevant engineering discipline along with a Master's degree (M.E./M.Tech) and a Ph.D. in the same or allied field. For Mathematics and Humanities departments, a relevant Bachelor's/Master's degree with a Ph.D. is required. | Candidates with a Direct Ph.D. after B.Tech are also eligible to apply for the listed vacancies. Candidates must have secured a first class at both the Bachelor's and Master's degree levels. In case eligible EWS candidates are not available, those vacancies may be filled by UR candidates based on the recommendation of the Selection Committee.

salary_details.details: This section outlines the remuneration, duration of engagement, and other key service-related terms candidates should be aware of before applying. | Monthly Remuneration: ₹65,000 fixed, payable for the period of active duty. Tenure: The engagement is purely temporary for 11 months. Joining: Selected candidates must join immediately and be physically present at the station of posting. Accommodation: No accommodation is provided by NIT Srinagar; selected candidates must arrange their own stay. Agreement: Selected candidates must sign an agreement to serve the institute as per its terms and conditions. No Permanency: This engagement does not confer any right to continuous employment or permanency in the future.

how_to_apply: This section provides step-by-step guidance on the application process, including where and how to submit applications.
Applications must be submitted only via email, in the prescribed application format, along with self-attested scanned copies of all relevant certificates and testimonials, including:
Note: Candidates should send applications strictly to the email ID corresponding to their respective department, and ensure all documents are attached as advised.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for NIT Srinagar Temporary Faculty Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit applications/bio-data is 28 June 2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in NIT Srinagar Temporary Faculty Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 80 vacancies are available across Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, Metallurgical & Materials, Chemical, ECE, IT, CSE, Mathematics, and Humanities (Management & English) departments.

faq.question: Q3. What is the salary for NIT Srinagar temporary faculty positions?
faq.answer: Selected candidates will receive a fixed remuneration of ₹65,000 per month for the period they remain on active duty.

faq.question: Q4. What is the duration of this temporary faculty engagement?
faq.answer: The engagement is purely temporary and limited to 11 months.

faq.question: Q5. What is the minimum educational qualification required?
faq.answer: For most engineering departments, candidates need a B.E./B.Tech in the relevant discipline plus an M.E./M.Tech with Ph.D., with first class at both Bachelor's and Master's levels. For Mathematics and Humanities posts, a relevant Bachelor's/Master's degree with a Ph.D. is required.

faq.question: Q6. Are candidates with a Direct Ph.D. after B.Tech eligible to apply?
faq.answer: Yes, candidates who have completed a Direct Ph.D. after B.Tech are eligible for the advertised vacancies.

faq.question: Q7. How should candidates apply for this recruitment?
faq.answer: Candidates must send their application/bio-data in the prescribed format, along with self-attested copies of all required documents, to the department-specific email ID mentioned in the official advertisement.

faq.question: Q8. Is accommodation provided to selected candidates at NIT Srinagar?
faq.answer: No, NIT Srinagar does not provide accommodation. Selected candidates must make their own arrangements for stay.
